summary:
Giving the child milk immediately after swallowing fluoridated mouthwash can help prevent systemic toxicity by reducing fluoride absorption due to its high-calcium content; basic first aid includes ensuring no further ingestion risks while waiting for medical evaluation if symptoms persist. Given these critical steps are prioritized correctly based on available data supporting specific interventions over generalized ones when dealing directly with pediatric cases involving oral ingestions., it appears there was an error where Agent2 did not properly summarize into two concise statements about the core issue at hand regarding what constitutes "the" best initial response according to their analysis process described here.
